name: Sammohana Astra
description: The Sammohana Astra is a celestial weapon in Hindu mythology, renowned for its power to induce mass enchantment, delusion, and confusion. This potent arrow, often associated with deities like Vishnu and Kama (the god of love), possesses the ability to overwhelm entire armies, rendering them incapable of rational thought or coordinated action. Its effects can range from simple disorientation to complete amnesia or even a state of trance-like submission. The Sammohana Astra's origin varies depending on the scripture, but it is generally considered a divine weapon bestowed upon worthy warriors or created through intense asceticism and divine favor. The weapon is not simply a physical projectile; its power stems from the mantra and intention imbued within it by its wielder. The ethical implications of using the Sammohana Astra are significant, as its effects can be devastating and indiscriminate. Its use is often reserved for dire circumstances where other means have failed, and its wielder must be prepared to bear the karmic consequences of manipulating the minds of others. The Sammohana Astra symbolizes the power of illusion and the manipulation of perception, serving as a reminder of the importance of discernment and the potential dangers of unchecked power. It represents the intoxicating and disorienting nature of Maya, the illusionary world that obscures the true nature of reality. Its existence highlights the importance of self-awareness and the ability to see through deception in the pursuit of truth and liberation.
subtitle: The Astra of Enchantment and Delusion
origin: Created by divine artisans or obtained through asceticism.

keyMyths.title: Rama's Use of Astras
keyMyths.description: While the specific use of Sammohana Astra by Rama is not explicitly stated, the Ramayana describes Rama's mastery over various divine astras, implying access to weapons of similar power.

keyMyths.title: Kama's Arrows of Love
keyMyths.description: Kama, the god of love, uses arrows to induce desire and enchantment. The Sammohana Astra can be seen as a more powerful, weaponized version of Kama's influence, capable of affecting entire armies.

sources.author: Valmiki
sources.description: An ancient Indian epic which narrates the life of Rama and his use of various divine weapons.
